vocab_items.sentence: Her friends often tease her about it, but she doesn’t mind.
vocab_items.explanation: To make fun of someone in a playful or unkind way.

vocab_items.sentence: she walks to school, to work, and even to the market when she needs groceries.
vocab_items.explanation: Food and other items that you buy at a supermarket or market.

vocab_items.sentence: She doesn’t get tired easily and finds walking very relaxing.
vocab_items.explanation: Helping you to rest and feel less stressed or worried.

vocab_items.sentence: She didn’t have an umbrella, so she decided to run home.
vocab_items.explanation: A tool used to keep you dry when it is raining.

vocab_items.sentence: Veronica realized that she was enjoying it.
vocab_items.explanation: To suddenly understand or become aware of something.

vocab_items.sentence: Her friends often tease her about it, but she doesn’t mind.
vocab_items.explanation: To be annoyed or worried by something.

vocab_items.sentence: she enjoys walking for long hours.
vocab_items.explanation: To continue the activity of walking for a period of time.

vocab_items.sentence: her stomach always hurts when she runs.
vocab_items.explanation: To cause pain at a specific moment.

vocab_items.sentence: Veronica doesn’t plan to buy a car.
She doesn’t even want to learn how to drive.
vocab_items.explanation: To gain knowledge or skill on how to perform a task.

vocab_items.sentence: Veronica was waiting at the bus stop.
vocab_items.explanation: To stay in a place until something arrives.

vocab_items.sentence: she decided to run home, even though she hated running.
vocab_items.explanation: To move quickly toward your house.

vocab_items.sentence: When she finally reached home, she was smiling.
vocab_items.explanation: To arrive at your house after traveling.
